content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 30, 2003

Gail C. Fry
2010 West Avenue K, Suite 616
Lancaster, CA 93536

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-087

Dear Ms. Fry:

We are in receipt of your April 23, 2003, letter requesting assistance under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ The Fair Political Practices Commission may only provide formal written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Act or to their authorized representation. (Government Code Section 83114(b).) Regulation 18329(b)(2) specifies:

"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the following requirements are met:
(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such authorization has been made."

Furthermore, should this inquiry be hypothetical in nature, regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), states that this is a circumstance in which the FPPC may decline to provide written advice. However, if your inquiry applies to a specific official please contact us again with that individual’s consent. I enclose a copy of

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

regulation 18329 for your review.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5660 should you have any additional questions.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosure
JWW:jg
